other_observers: Rachel Hopper; Not sure who original observer was. Dixie Smith had reported the bird from that location a few days prior to our arrival.
description: Almost immediately upon arriving at Rist Benson Reservoir, I picked the Great Black-backed Gull swimming. Soon thereafter, the bird swam to a pod of other gulls along the shore. Compared to nearby Ring-billed and Herring Gulls, the bird was noticeably larger in all aspects with a bulky body, thick neck, and very heavy bill. The upperparts were almost jet-black, though were a slight shade lighter than the primaries. The bill was mostly yellow, except for a red gonys and a small black edge to that gonys. The legs were a pale fleshy pink. The head, neck, breast, and underparts were clean white. The tertial and scapular crescents were white and the black primaries displayed large white mirrors.Behaviors: Noted swimming and foraging along shore.Call: None heardPlumage: Definitive alternate
photographer: Photographer: Larry Semo, Larry Semo, Larry Semo, Larry Semo, Rachel Hopper
